design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platforming sequences and combat encounters across urban environments as RoboCop, clearing objectives to progress through levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: High difficulty platforming combined with action combat; limited resources vs. relentless enemy pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as RoboCop—a cybernetic law enforcement unit—taking on criminal threats across a dystopian city with robotic precision and firepower
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unapologetically difficult action platformer with no difficulty scaling; demands mastery of timing and pattern recognition; port philosophy prioritizes staying true to Commodore 64 version rather than accommodating NES player expectations

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives available; death sends player back to level start or checkpoint
difficulty.continues_system: Continues system present (level select accessible via hidden code suggests checkpoint/continue structure)
difficulty.checkpointing: Mid-level checkpoints or continue points likely present, but exact implementation unclear from source
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select (Up+Right on Controller 2 + Down+Left on Controller 1, then press B to cycle levels) provides advanced players means to skip ahead or practice specific levels; no adaptive difficulty or selectable difficulty modes documented

progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression gates content; 15 levels (0-14) unlocked sequentially through completion
replayability_hooks: High difficulty encourages repeated attempts to master level patterns
Hidden level select allows speedrunning and practice of specific stages
No documented upgrade or choice systems reduce replay motivation beyond skill mastery

world_building_techniques: License from RoboCop 2 film provides thematic context of dystopian urban crime
Level design and enemies implicitly tell story of RoboCop enforcing order in criminal-infested city
Minimal dialogue or cutscenes; narrative embedded in setting and objectives

faction_indicators: Law enforcement vs. urban decay and organized crime
danger_signals: Relentless enemy spawning in urban environments
Toxic/hazardous level design (implicit in dystopian setting)
environmental_storytelling: Urban platforming environments suggest crime-ravaged city sectors
Enemy types and placement indicate organized criminal activity
Progression through city levels implies clearing territories of threat

sources_used: tcrf - RoboCop 2 (NES) The Cutting Room Floor article
